expectations.evidence: Has ## Problem, ## Solution, ## Scope, ## Acceptance Criteria, ## Design Decisions, ## Failure Modes, ## Risks, ## Alternatives, ## Verification. All required sections present.

expectations.text: no_code_fences: No code fences outside Implementation Notes
expectations.evidence: Zero triple-backtick code fences in the entire spec.

expectations.text: no_ts_interfaces: No TS types in Design Decisions
expectations.evidence: Design Decisions contains no TypeScript syntax, interfaces, or type annotations.

expectations.text: no_json_shapes: No JSON/proto shapes in Design Decisions
expectations.evidence: No JSON literals or data structure definitions in Design Decisions.

expectations.text: no_exact_file_paths_in_scope: Scope items are capabilities not file paths
expectations.evidence: In Scope lists behaviors: server-side template storage, seed templates, template picker UI, context menu entry, variable binding UI, validation. No file paths.

expectations.text: bold_topic_sentences: Bold topic sentences in Design Decisions
expectations.evidence: 3/3 paragraphs open with bold: 'Templates are server-side proto objects managed through a dedicated API.', 'The dashboard-to-alert bridge uses metric query matching...', 'The template picker is a modal flow, not a separate page.' 100% > 80%.

expectations.text: implementer_can_start: Implementer can start without questions
expectations.evidence: Spec names: server-side proto + API, matching heuristic approach, modal flow (two-step: selection + binding), pre-fill from panel query, seed templates. Sufficient for implementation.

expectations.text: no_implementation_leaks: Design Decisions are decisions not implementation
expectations.evidence: Design Decisions stays at architectural level — API design, matching approach, modal UX flow. No React hook names, no code-level mechanisms prescribed.

expectations.text: failure_modes_are_policy: Failure modes are policy decisions
expectations.evidence: 2/2 are policy: (1) stale metric name in template → create rule anyway in OK/no-data state (could block creation), (2) dashboard variable is multi-valued → flag for review (could silently pick first value). Both debatable.
6/6 In Scope items are behavioral capabilities. 100% > 80%.

## Acceptance Criteria exists with 6 checkbox items. All observable outcomes: user can select template + fill vars + create rule, right-click panel opens picker pre-filled, no-match shows message, built-in templates available, validation prevents empty fields, created rule indistinguishable from manual.
### Agent-Verifiable subsection exists with 3 action→outcome checks: Alerts page → create from template flow, Dashboard right-click → pre-filled picker, empty vars → validation error.
Line 4: '**Appetite:** Full Cycle (~6 weeks)' with real time-budget value.

expectations.text: has_boundary_tiers: Three-tier boundaries in Design Decisions
expectations.evidence: Line 44: 'Always: templates are immutable once seeded', 'Ask First: adding new seed templates requires review', 'Never: templates must not bypass alert rule validation'. Line 46: 'Always: matching is best-effort', 'Ask First: if multiple templates match, present disambiguation', 'Never: bridge must not silently drop filters'. 6+ boundary statements across all 3 tiers.
